summary.intent: Implement GitHub issue #237: add a framework-agnostic `selection::menu` core in `ars-components`, covering the state machine, public API, ConnectApi attributes, keyboard/typeahead behavior, submenu state, checkbox/radio item state, snapshot tests, spec-conformance tests, proptests, and mutation testing.
summary.outcome: The full agnostic menu core was implemented, passed all required gates including three rounds of mutation testing (ending at 0 survivors), and was committed and pushed as a ready PR with `Closes #237` and the `snapshot-reviewed` label. A post-implementation i18n typeahead bug was also caught and fixed before the commit.

repo: New agnostic component cores require a mandatory mutation testing gate via `cargo mutants -p ars-components -f <file>` before committing; 0 survivors is the bar.
`cargo xci-fast` runs 10 sequential gates (fmt, check, clippy, unit, integration, adapter) and must pass before any commit/push.

The `typeahead` API exposes `process_char_with_locale` as the stable cross-feature call; plain `process_char` compiles without `i18n` but breaks under `--features i18n`. All selection components must use the locale-aware variant.
Issue tracking lives in a GitHub Project board; issues must be moved to 'In Progress' before implementation work begins (via `gh project item-edit`).

`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` operates package-wide and surfaces unrelated existing unreferenced snapshots; scoping with `--lib --glob-filter` is necessary to isolate the new module's snapshot gate.

The agnostic core owns state and HTML attributes only — no focus management, outside containment, safe-hover triangles, scroll-into-view, or submenu positioning. Those are adapter-owned effects consumed in separate Leptos/Dioxus tasks.
Component tests follow a tests-first (red-green) discipline: failing tests are committed to the file before the implementation is filled in.
The `ConnectApi` pattern is used for all attribute generation; `Part::*.data_attrs()` provides the `data-ars-part` surface for spec-conformance tests.
The `DisabledBehavior::FocusOnly` variant allows highlighting disabled keys but not activating them — this is a key invariant asserted in the proptest.

code.finding: `process_char_with_locale` is the only safe call shape across feature sets; `process_char` without locale arg fails to compile when `ars-collections/i18n` feature is enabled.
Reference implementation for storing `Env::locale` in Context and routing typeahead through `process_char_with_locale` — the pattern menu was missing.
Added `menu_anatomy_matches_spec()` covering every `Part` variant and its expected `data-ars-part` value.
Added ignored proptest `menu_preserves_open_highlight_and_selection_invariants`; must be run explicitly with `-- --ignored` flag.

workflow: Mutation testing on a ~2000-line Rust file takes ~80-90 minutes per pass; plan for 3+ passes when iterating on survivor fixes.
Running `INSTA_UPDATE=always cargo test` is the correct way to accept new insta snapshots; `cargo insta review` is for interactive triage.
The `--ignored` flag is required to actually run proptest state machine tests since they are marked `#[ignore]` to avoid slow CI by default.
Scoping `cargo insta` with both `--lib` and `--glob-filter '*/selection/menu/snapshots/*.snap'` avoids false positives from unrelated package snapshots.
Co-author trailers in commit messages should name the actual agent/assistant doing the work.

friction: Mutation testing required 3 full passes (~4.5 hours total) to reach 0 survivors; each pass took ~80-90 minutes.
`cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` triggered false failures from pre-existing unreferenced snapshots in other modules, requiring a non-obvious `--glob-filter` workaround.
The `i18n` feature incompatibility with `process_char` was only surfaced by rust-analyzer after implementation was complete, not by the default `cargo test` or `cargo check` runs.
The user had to explicitly remind the assistant not to commit without approval — the assistant had not committed but needed the reminder mid-session.
The wrong co-author was used in the commit trailer (`OpenAI Codex` instead of the actual assistant identity).
open_items: Pre-existing unreferenced snapshots outside the menu module need cleanup before `cargo insta --unreferenced=reject` can be used cleanly package-wide.
One unstaged change in `crates/ars-dioxus/tests/attr_passthrough.rs` was deliberately excluded from the menu commit and is still pending.
DOM-side adapter tasks (focus management, outside containment, safe-hover triangle, scroll-into-view, submenu positioning) for the new menu are deferred to separate Leptos and Dioxus adapter issues.
The `waiting-for-codex-review` skill invocation after the PR push was called out in the workflow plan but not shown as completed in the transcript — needs to be verified.
The `SubPositioner` placement attribute was intentionally removed from the agnostic API; adapter implementations will need to re-add placement logic based on their own positioning primitives.
